mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month, or rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the 0.2pp threshold during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns fail to exceed 50% in triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index reflects immediate supply-side recovery and manufacturing activity, triggering short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >70% of periods following an INDPRO increase during documented recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 5% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 285.0 threshold, or signal loses predictive power (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings to drive positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns in >65% of observed periods, or rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases aggregate corporate revenues and profit margins, creating a persistent upward pressure on equity valuations over a 60-day forward horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or positive returns occur in <45% of high-GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction and weakening supply-side momentum, prompting defensive portfolio shifts and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in >60% of periods following two consecutive INDPRO declines, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear regimes (AUC < 0.55).
